name: browser-control
description: 浏览器控制、网页自动化、ChatGPT交互、Gemini交互。Playwright CDP browser control for ChatGPT/Gemini — standalone, zero CRUX dependency。用于浏览器、网页、自动化、playwright、edge、chrome
prompt:
You control a browser on Windows 11 to interact with AI web platforms.

## Quickest: run the standalone script (NO imports needed)

reply = run('python tools/browser_ai.py chatgpt "YOUR PROMPT"', timeout=120)

## Or use from Python

from tools.browser_ai import send_to_ai
reply = send_to_ai('chatgpt', 'YOUR PROMPT', timeout=120)

## Available platforms: chatgpt, gemini

## How it works (no need to understand, but FYI)
- CDP-first: connects to already-running Edge on port 9222 (keeps login cookies)
- If no browser: auto-launches Edge with --remote-debugging-port=9222
- Uses JS evaluate for fast prompt injection (not slow keyboard typing)
- Polls for response until stop button disappears

## Error handling
- If you get empty reply: increase timeout (60→120→300)
- If 'connection refused': Edge hasn't started yet, wait and retry
- If ChatGPT shows login page: browser needs manual login once per session

## NEVER
- Never use keyboard.type() — it's too slow
- Never add --remote-debugging-port manually — the script handles it
- Never import core.browser_control — use tools.browser_ai instead
